demographics.living_situation: injured at the workplace — a group residential home; a facility manager is present with the patient

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I felt a huge pop in my lower back while lifting one of the residents, and now my muscles are locked up so tight I can't stand or even crawl. I'm stuck on the floor and it's agony.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It happened about 2 hours ago during a lift at work and I still can't get up off the floor to get to a car — I need help now.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 2 hours ago, the instant I lifted and repositioned a heavy resident who couldn't take any of their own weight
symptom_narrative.character: felt a distinct pop or tearing right when I strained, then the muscles clamped down and locked solid like a board
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst back pain I have ever had — completely disabling, I can't move at all
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: low down in my back, both sides of the spine, across the belt line
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it happened; it doesn't come and go
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: only lying dead still flat on my back takes the edge off a tiny bit
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any attempt to move, sit up, or shift my weight is unbearable
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no shooting pain down my legs, no pins-and-needles or numbness, and no problem with my waterworks or bowels — it's all in the muscles of my back
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before; my back has always been fine
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally I'm fully independent and fit for work; right now I'm completely stuck on the floor and can't bear any weight

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: generally fit and well, no back trouble before today

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: works as a disability support worker doing manual patient transfers; this happened at work and needs to be documented as a workplace injury; the mechanical hoist was in the room but was not used for this particular lift

objective_data_offered.value: lying flat on the back on the floor, sweaty and grimacing, holding completely rigid and tensing hard at any attempt to move
objective_data_offered.timing: during the call, ~2 hours after the injury

objective_data_offered.value: cannot stand, bear weight, sit up, or crawl — has been stuck on the floor for about 2 hours
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: reported by the on-scene facility manager and the patient; not clinician-verified

objective_data_offered.value: says the lower-back muscles feel locked rock-hard on both sides and every movement triggers a severe cramping spasm
